entries.preview:
# git — Branch operations

List all branches: git branch -a
Create branch: git branch <name>
Create and switch: git checkout -b <name>  or  git switch -c <name>
Switch branch: git checkout <name>  o

# git — Commit operations

Commit message must be in quotes: git commit -m "message"
Stage files first if nothing is staged: git add <file>
Empty commit: git commit --allow-empty -m "message"
Change

# git — Diff and comparison

Unstaged changes: git diff
Staged changes: git diff --cached
Compare branches: git diff main..feature-branch
Compare commits: git diff <hash1> <hash2>
Show only changed

# git — Log and history

Short log: git log --oneline -20
Graph view: git log --oneline --graph --all
File history: git log --follow -p -- <file>
Filter by author: git log --author="Name"
Filter by

# git — Merge and rebase operations

Merge: git merge <branch>
Squash merge: git merge --squash <branch>
No fast-forward merge: git merge --no-ff <branch>
Abort merge: git merge --abort
After resolv

# git — Pull and fetch operations

Pull with merge: git pull origin <branch>
Pull with rebase: git pull --rebase origin <branch>
After conflicts: git rebase --continue or git rebase --abort
Fetch on

# git — Push operations

Push new branch for the first time: git push -u origin <branch-name>
Push existing branch: git push origin <branch-name>
Force push (safe): git push --force-with-lease origi

# git — Remote and tag operations

List remotes: git remote -v
Add remote: git remote add origin <url>
Change remote URL: git remote set-url origin <url>
Remove remote: git remote remove <name>
Rena

# git — Reset and undo operations

Soft reset (undo commit, keep changes staged): git reset --soft HEAD~1
Mixed reset (undo commit, changes become unstaged): git reset HEAD~1
Hard reset (CAUTION —

# git — Stash operations

Save changes: git stash push -m "description"
Include untracked files: git stash push -u -m "description"
List stashes: git stash list
Apply (keep stash): git stash apply s

# stanctl — Self-monitoring agent management

[agent-apply]
Installs or updates the Instana self-monitoring agent.
Usage: stanctl agent apply [flags]
Alias: stanctl ag a
Mode: --agent-mode INFRASTRU

# stanctl — Air-gapped (offline/disconnected) environment

[air-gapped-package]
Creates the air-gapped package (run on a machine with internet access).
Usage: stanctl air-gapped package [flags]
Alia

# stanctl — Authentication management

[create-admin-api-token]
Creates an admin API token.
Usage: stanctl auth create-admin-api-token

[reset-2fa]
Resets two-factor authentication for a user.
Usage

# stanctl — Backend and datastore management

[backend-apply]
Installs or updates the Instana backend (use when cluster is already running).
Usage: stanctl backend apply [flags]
Alias: stanctl be a

# stanctl — Cluster management (up, down, create, delete, backup, restore)

[up]
Creates the cluster or starts a stopped cluster.
Usage: stanctl up [flags]
Required (initial setup): --core-base-doma

# stanctl — Debug, benchmark and dump

[debug]
Creates a debug package (logs, manifests, system info, healthchecks, metrics, config).
Usage: stanctl debug [-o <dir>] [-n <namespaces>]
Output: -o, --

# stanctl — License management

[license-download]
Downloads the Instana license file.
Usage: stanctl license download [--sales-key <key>] [-o <dir>]
Alias: stanctl lc
Output: -o, --output-dir (defa

# stanctl — Version and image management

[versions-identify]
Lists Instana versions compatible with the current installation.
Usage: stanctl versions identify [flags]
Alias: stanctl v id
Registry a
